text.title: A/B Testing Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped A/B runtimes. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Same user sees variant A on web and variant B on mobile in the same session; conversion attribution scrambled.

root-cause.source: Microsoft ExP guidance + Internal — A/B audits 2024

antipattern.root-cause: Bucket function used random.choice / Math.random instead of a hash of user_id + experiment_id.
antipattern.fix: Replace assignment with 64-bit hash of (user_id || experiment_id || salt) mod 100. Pin salt per experiment. See rule deterministic-hash.

antipattern.symptom: Experiment "wins" with p=0.02; observed split was 47/53 instead of 50/50; ship causes a regression.

root-cause.source: Kohavi et al. — Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments

antipattern.root-cause: SRM not checked; bucket leak or filter on one arm silently biases the result.
antipattern.fix: Compute chi-square p-value of observed split vs expected; if p < 0.001 mark decision=invalid-srm and refuse to ship. See rule srm-gate.

antipattern.symptom: Team kills an experiment at 30% of sample size after a "significant" result; the lift was noise.
antipattern.root-cause: No sequential-test correction; team treats fixed-horizon p-value as live-monitor.
antipattern.fix: Lock decision until planned sample size reached, or use sequential-test (mSPRT) with adjusted alpha. See rule wilson-ci-not-percent.

antipattern.symptom: Mid-experiment, targeting rule changes; old buckets now exposed to a different variant; data invalid.
antipattern.root-cause: Targeting changes during the experiment without re-assignment isolation.
antipattern.fix: Freeze the assignment salt + targeting rule at experiment start; any change ends the experiment and starts a new one. See rule sticky-across-surfaces.
